spine-east.name: The Spine -- East End
spine-east.description:
You climb a crumbling on-ramp onto The Spine -- an elevated highway stretching west across the wasteland. Rusted vehicles litter both lanes, baking in the heat. The asphalt radiates warmth through your boots.

Endless scrubland dotted with ruins under a hazy {dim:amber-grey} sky. To the west, the highway stretches onward. Behind you, Dusthaven is a smudge on the horizon.

examine.vehicles: Cars and trucks frozen mid-journey, picked clean by scavengers. A few still have personal belongings visible through dirty windows -- snapshots of the moment everything stopped.
examine.view: Flat scrubland with occasional ruins. Far to the west, a tall structure rises above the horizon -- the antenna.
examine.road: Four lanes of cracked asphalt. Weeds push through the gaps. Built to last, and it has -- barely.

overpass.name: Overpass Junction
overpass.description:
The highway crosses a collapsed interchange. Part of the road has fallen away, leaving a {pink:dangerous gap} with twisted rebar and rubble below. Wind whistles through the opening.

Someone has laid a {cyan:makeshift bridge} across the gap using car hoods and rope. It looks... functional.

examine.bridge: Car hoods lashed together with wire and rope. It sways when you test it. Not reassuring, but it'll hold.
examine.gap: A three-meter gap. Below, a 15-meter drop into rubble. Not survivable.
examine.below: Collapsed concrete and rusted vehicles. Something moves down there -- animals, probably. Or something else.

choices.resultText: You edge across. It creaks and sways, but holds. Your heart rate doesn't return to normal for several minutes.
There's no other way across. The bridge is it.

wreckage.name: Vehicle Wreckage
wreckage.description:
A massive pileup blocks the road -- dozens of vehicles fused together in a wall of rust and broken glass. Forty years old, but you can still read the panic in the arrangement. The air reeks of old oil and corroded metal.

A narrow path has been cleared through the wreckage. It's {pink:tight and dark} inside.

encounter.introText: As you squeeze through the wreckage, a figure drops from the top of the bus. {pink:A bandit}, armed with a length of pipe. 'Hand over your supplies, or this gets ugly.'

bus.text: A city bus, windows smashed. Inside, personal items litter the floor -- bags, a child's toy, a phone with a dead battery. On one seat, a {purple:data cache} blinks faintly, powered by a tiny solar cell.

examine.vehicles: Everyone tried to flee at once. The highway became a parking lot, then a graveyard. Some doors are still open, as if the occupants walked away on foot.
examine.path: A narrow channel cut through by scavengers. Just wide enough for one person. Dark enough to hide an ambush.

spine-west.name: The Spine -- West End
spine-west.description:
The highway opens up past the wreckage. A junction ahead: one branch continues west toward the off-ramp. The other curves {dim:northwest} but is completely collapsed -- impassable.

The {purple:signal in your implant} is noticeably stronger here. Your hands are tingling.

examine.fork: The northwest branch is gone -- a 50-meter section collapsed into rubble. West is the only option.

signal.text: Your {purple:implant} reads: {bright:SIGNAL BEARING: 274 WEST -- STRENGTH 6/10}. Getting close. The signal has a rhythm to it now -- almost like breathing.

spine-exit.name: Spine Off-Ramp
spine-exit.description:
The off-ramp descends into a flat industrial zone. Warehouses and server buildings stretch ahead -- the old tech district. A sign, barely legible: {dim:SECTOR 7-G -- ATLAS INFRASTRUCTURE CORP}.

The {purple:signal} pulls you forward like a current.

examine.sign: ATLAS INFRASTRUCTURE CORP -- SECTOR 7-G -- AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY. The restriction seems quaint now.
examine.buildings: Low concrete buildings, most with caved-in roofs. Server farms, data centers, networking hubs. Where ATLAS's physical infrastructure lived.
